name: Copper Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons melted butter or vegetable oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Peel the garlic cloves.
3. In a small bowl, combine the salt, pepper, and 1 tablespoon of paprika.
4. Rub the chicken with the butter or oil, then coat it evenly with the spice mixture.
5. Stuff the cavity of the bird with the remaining garlic cloves, half of the orange peel, bay leaves, and rosemary sprigs.
6. Truss the legs and place the chicken in a large baking dish. Refrigerate for 3 to 4 hours, well wrapped.
7. Bring the chilled chicken to room temperature before cooking.
8. Add enough stock to reach about 1 inch up the side of the chicken.
9. Place the chicken in the oven and bake for 1 to 1 1/2 hours, or until a meat thermometer inserted into the thigh registers 180 degrees Fahrenheit.
10. Transfer the chicken to a cutting board and let rest for 10 minutes before carving.
11. Add any remaining stock to the pan, return it to medium-high heat, and stir, scraping cooked bits from the bottom of the pan.
12. Add the remaining orange peel, chopped rosemary, and 1/2 teaspoon paprika; season with salt and pepper to taste.
13. Continue boiling until the sauce is reduced by about one-third, approximately 5 to 10 minutes.
14. Arrange the carved chicken on a serving platter and pass a bowl of the sauce separately.
Yield: 4-6 servings
